nature-hills.title: Autumn Royalty® Encore® Azalea
nature-hills.body_html:
With a robust, upright form that grows larger than other Azaleas, the dark green foliage and large, richly colored blooms of Encore® Autumn Royalty® Azalea (Rhododendron 'Conlec') dazzle visitors in your landscape. This is a hard-working variety that delivers up to 3 flushes of blooms a year.

Not only is the plant itself the largest Encore® Azalea, but even the 6-inch oval evergreen leaves and 4-inch flowers are also a bit bigger than other varieties! You'll really appreciate seeing the multiple waves of lavender purple blooms in early spring as they keep having encores of blooms, mid-summer and again in the fall. With bright light purple, funnel-shaped blooms cloaking it, the Encore® Azalea delivers an impressive performance. Butterflies and Hummingbirds are drawn to the nectar source!

Encore® Autumn Royalty® Azalea won "Rhododendron of the Year" by the Rhododendron Society of America and once you plant it, you'll understand why! Autumn Royalty® not only provides an abundance of blooms but does so with little or no care from you. You can just plant it in the right spot and sit back to watch the show! Hardy throughout USDA growing zones 6 to 9, these broad-leaved evergreens grow 4-5 feet in height and 3-4 feet wide.

Planting and Application:

This beautiful plant is known for its repeating blooms and sun tolerance. Autumn Royalty® thrives in the all-day sun with mid-afternoon shade. It's a versatile size for most landscapes and can be used in many different garden applications.

A single plant can be a spectacular evergreen specimen anywhere you want to add color from spring through fall. You'll highlight the repeat bloom to perfection this way. Why not make a marvelous focal point by mixing Autumn Royalty® with an Encore® Autumn Twist® and that deep, delicious purple of Polarnacht? White flowering varieties also look fabulous with Autumn Royalty®!

Because of the low maintenance, carefree nature of this shrub, this is an ideal plant for mass plantings in either commercial or home landscapes. They are wonderfully mass planted as a backdrop for smaller varieties. Create a solid screen by planting 3 feet apart on center. You'll measure from the center of one to the center of the next. Use several of them to create a friendly fence. Use them as an informal flowering hedge along your property line. Or, use a short row of them right behind your patio seating to give a welcome sense of privacy.

Use them as outstanding specimen plants by themselves, or to create a beautiful backdrop. After all, even in winter evergreen Autumn Royalty® continues to provide a lively presence in your yard. Use as an understory plant at the edge of a woodland planting or under large shade trees. Just ensure they get at least 4 hours of sunlight a day.

They are a perfect shrub to use in large containers to decorate your patio or porch. Your style can shine through in the choice of containers you use. Go for a modern look with tall, spare rectangular pots in 3 different sizes. Or, make it really personal with a bold mix of glazed containers in your favorite color.

  • Larger Growing Azalea Variety
  • Voted Azalea of the Year by the American Rhododendron Society
  • Repeat Blooming, Large Rich Purple Blooms
  • Fast Growing Evergreen Deep Green Foliage
  • Can Be Planted Underneath Taller Trees
  • Magnificent in Mass Plantings

#ProPlantTips for Care:

Encore® Autumn Royalty® broad-leaved evergreen Azalea will make you look good. And it doesn't ask for much in return.

Give them a planting spot that receives full sun and then shaded conditions from mid-afternoon onward, especially in the warmest growing zones. Choose a location with good airflow to help avoid spring frost damage to the flower buds.

Plant in soil that is well drained. Azaleas will simply not tolerate any long periods in water. If poor drainage is suspected, use a raised bed or plant on a sloping planting site. Another wonderful option to improve drainage would be to mound up to avoid root rot by planting high in a mounded heap of soil 18 to 24 inches above the lay of your land.

If you don't get adequate rainfall, please provide a moderate amount of water on a regular basis for the first season as the roots get established in your native soil. After that, the plants display some drought tolerance but water them well every so often during extended dry periods.

Use a thin layer of pine straw mulch if you can find it in your area. Keep all mulch several inches away from the main trunk for proper air circulation. Azaleas really prefer slightly acidic soils. Have a soil test done to make sure your soil is between 5.0 and 5.5. You'll want to amend the soil when needed, and in cases of high pH, add Dr. Earth Acid Lovers Planting Mix to 1/3rd of the total volume of backfill when planting. Watch for chlorosis (yellowing leaves) always the first sign of a pH problem. Fertilize in the late fall and mid-summer with Dr. Earth Acid Lovers Fertilizer to help maintain pH.

Autumn Royalty® needs little care or pruning and grows naturally beautiful but will accept some pruning if you so desire. Prune to shape immediately after the first spring flowering. You can control for size and remove broken branches at that time.

  • Full Sun & Partial Shade
  • Moderate to Moist Soil
  • Well-Drained Very Enriched Soil
  • Prune Right After Flowering
  • Year-Round Foliage!
  • Heat & Cold Tolerant
  • Deer & Rabbit Resistant

Order this hot variety today! We sell out almost as fast as the new crop is put online. If you see it in stock, go ahead and place your order. You'll never regret this choice. Order the Encore® Autumn Royalty® Azalea for your landscape today from NatureHills.com!

planting-tree.title: Autumn Royalty® Encore® Azalea
planting-tree.tags: Bloom Season_Fall, Bloom Season_Spring, Bloom Season_Summer, Brand_Encore Azalea, Category_Azaleas, Category_Disease Resistant, Category_Evergreen Shrubs, Category_Flowering Shrubs, Category_Low Maintenance, Category_Others and Types, Category_Shrubs, Drought Tolerance_Good, Fall Color_Green, Grows in Zones_10, Grows in Zones_6, Grows in Zones_7, Grows in Zones_8, Grows in Zones_9, Growth Rate_moderate, Mature Height_4-5 Feet, Mature Width_4-5 Feet, Shipping Restriction_AK, Shipping Restriction_AZ, Shipping Restriction_HI, Soil Type_Acidic, Soil Type_Adaptable, Sunlight_Full, Sunlight_Partial
planting-tree.body_html:
Introduction

Unique Purple Color!!!

  • Year round color
  • Re-blooming purple flowers in spring and summer
  • Great for adding color to your landscape
  • Pest and disease resistant
  • Low maintenance

Never Bareroot... All plants come rooted into a container with soil.

Description

The Autumn Royalty® Encore® Azalea is not your normal everyday azalea. It has all the great characteristics of the original azalea and so much more! With this shrub producing flowers for longer periods of time, it is a staple for every landscape design.

The flowers produced are a beautiful, deep, jewel toned fuchsia. During the flowering seasons, the trumpet shaped flowers grow in clusters at the end of each branch and blanket the entire shrub. From spring to summer you can enjoy enchanting pink-purple petals for the entire warm season. The Autumn Royalty® Encore® Azalea will grow to a height and width of 4-5 feet. This is an evergreen shrub, meaning that foliage is present year round.

The Autumn Royalty® Encore® Azalea is a compact shrub that fills in those smaller spaces in your landscape. In the spring and summer, a bounty of beautiful blooms are the highlight. During the fall and winter, the shrub is a beautiful backdrop to other flowering plants in your beds and gardens. Use the Autumn Royalty® Azalea as a foundation plant or even plant these in front of a privacy tree wall to add a pop of color to your landscape. Use it as a lone specimen or plant in a container for your porch.

This azalea is fairly heat tolerant and requires no maintenance once established. It is recommended to prune after the blooming season and fertilize in the early spring to enjoy a fully blossomed shrub during the growing seasons. Encore azaleas are hardy, evergreen shrubs that are perfect for adding color to your landscape!

Product Attachment>>> Encore Azalea Versatility Sheet

How To Care

When planting your azalea be sure you have the right location and conditions for your new plants to thrive.

Planting time: Spring and fall are ideal times to plant azaleas. However, if you avoid freezing and extremely hot temperatures you can plant any time of the year

Sun: Full sun to part shade. Encore azaleas will perform best with at least 4-6 hours of sun. While your Encore azalea will still grow and even bloom in shady areas they are less likely to rebloom and the blooms won't be as prolific.

Soil: Azaleas prefer acidic soil that drains well. If you have alkaline soil, be sure to amend it. To acidify your soil amend with compost, elemental sulfur, or use fertilizers specific for acid loving plants. Mulching with pine straw or pine bark will help over time as they begin to break down, but are not an immediate solution. To improve drainage in something like a heavy clay soil, add compost and be sure to loosen up the soil well before planting.

Fertilizer: Choose a balanced slow release fertilizer. Fertilize in late winter or early spring before flowering for best results.

Pruning: You don’t need to prune Encore azaleas, but pruning your flowering shrubs after they are done blooming can help encourage a bushier growth and renew an older plant. You can use pruning to control size, but don't prune of more than 1/3 of the plant for best results.

Landscape Ideas

The Autumn Royalty Encore Azalea adds vibrant color to the southern landscape and beyond. Azaleas are excellent for borders and mass plantings. This evergreen shrub jazzes up gardens and mixed beds. At 4 to 5 feet tall and wide at maturity this stunning azalea can even be used in foundation plantings. Try this flowering shrub in a container to add a bold pop of color to your entryway, porch, patio, or deck. For borders or hedges plant about 4 feet apart on center. Otherwise, plant about 6 feet apart to give your plants space in-between.


For additional options, be sure to browse our Azalea and Evergreen Shrub collection.

nature-hills.title: Encore® Autumn Twist® Azalea
nature-hills.body_html:
Bi-color flowers and year-round appeal! The joyful Encore® Autumn Twist® Azalea, (Rhododendron 'Conlep' PP12133), is an ornamental flowering broad-leaved evergreen shrub with delightful, two-tone blooms. The blooms begin in spring and continue on through summer and even into fall for an exceptionally long season of re-blooming color.

Your Encore® Autumn Twist® Azalea features a big display of early spring pinkish-white petals with purple striping. Each lovely blossom is about 3 inches across, varying from white to pink, pinkish-purple, and purple. Followed by smaller flushes throughout the remainder of the growing season. The dark green foliage provides an elegant backdrop for the vibrant color of the flowers and will maintain an evergreen presence in your yard through the cold winter months.

It has an upright growth rate with a rounded form. Encore® Azaleas are fast-growing, so you won't have to wait long to see its spectacular results. Hardy throughout USDA growing zones 6 to 9, these easy-to-grow shrubs reach 4-5 feet in height at maturity and spread 3-4 feet wide.

Planting and Application:

Autumn Twist® features year-round foliage that creates privacy and screening all year! Create outdoor garden rooms or private seating areas with rows or strategic groupings of these evergreen shrubs! They, of course, look great mixed with other Encore® Azaleas! Plant a few as a short hedge along a side building, or just one in your front yard for 3-season color that can't be beat!

This broadleaf evergreen also makes a superior hedge and foundation planting, providing four seasons of color. Use several of them to create a friendly fence. Because of the low maintenance, carefree nature of this shrub, this is an ideal plant for mass plantings in either commercial or home landscapes. They are wonderfully mass planted as a backdrop for smaller varieties. Create a solid screen by planting 3 feet apart on center. You'll measure from the center of one to the center of the next.

A single spring specimen lights up the landscape or brightens a tired mixed-shrub border. Autumn Twist® well in containers and large planters for adding height to garden beds, or accenting porches and patios in the dappled sun/shade! Butterflies and Hummingbirds will seek out these trumpet-shaped stars for their nectar.

  • Pinkish-White Petals & Pinkish Purple Stripe
  • Repeat Blooming Bi-Color Flowers
  • Fast Growing Broad-Leaved Evergreen
  • Wonderful Repeat Blooms Spring Through Fall!
  • Hedges, Containers, Accents & Anchors, Specimens & Four Season Color

#ProPlantTips for Care:

Handling full sun and partial shade, afternoon shade especially in the hottest of its favored growing zones, Azaleas love a highly-organic, well-drained and moist environment to grow in. Their shallow root systems appreciate light mulching over the soil surface but despise soggy conditions. Autumn Twist® is heat, disease, and drought tolerant so it'll certainly be a tough little shrub that you won't have to fuss over. Only prune flowering broad-leaved evergreen shrubs immediately after the flowers fade to avoid removing next year's spring display!

  • Full Sun & Partial Shade/Afternoon Shade
  • Enriched Well-Drained Organic Soil
  • Regular Moisture & Appreciates Mulched Beds
  • Heat & Drought Tolerant
  • Disease Resistant

As Azaleas go, it's hard to beat the Encore® series, and Encore® Autumn Twist® Azalea is one of the best. Hardy and evergreen with a re-blooming display of bi-color flowers, Autumn Twist® is sure to enliven your landscape not just in autumn, but all year round. Order today at Nature Hills Nursery!

planting-tree.title: Autumn Angel® Encore® Azalea
planting-tree.tags: Bloom Color_White, Bloom Season_Fall, Bloom Season_Spring, Bloom Season_Summer, Brand_Encore Azalea, Category_Azaleas, Category_Disease Resistant, Category_Encore Azaleas, Category_Evergreen Shrubs, Category_Flowering Shrubs, Category_Shrubs, Drought Tolerance_Good, Grows in Zones_10, Grows in Zones_6, Grows in Zones_7, Grows in Zones_8, Grows in Zones_9, Mature Height_2-3 Feet, Mature Width_2-3 Feet, Shipping Restriction_AK, Shipping Restriction_AZ, Shipping Restriction_HI, Soil Type_Acidic, Sunlight_Full, Sunlight_Partial
planting-tree.body_html:
Low Maintenance, Evergreen shrub that keeps blooming over and over again!

If you plan on planting a cluster of azaleas or are just looking to add color to your home, Encore® Azaleas are the perfect choice.

The Autumn Angel® Encore® Azalea displays tons of white blooms from spring all the way to fall! This evergreen azalea will look great all year around. Flowers are prominent for an extended growing season and fall and winter gives way to foliage that is still intact. No more naked azaleas in the winter. Not only does this shrub look great all year around, but it is also disease resistant, pest resistant, and heat tolerant.

Thinking about planting as a hedge? The Autumn Angel® will look amazing. How about a cool s-shaped hedge with some smaller plants to give a nice layering effect? Encore azaleas paired with Emerald Arborvitae for height can give your home a nice warm look. Place 2 Emerald Arborvitae on each side of the windows and a couple of encore azaleas under the window. This is a simple design that is low maintenance. Check out our Landscape Designers gallery for some landscape ideas.

Product Attachment >>> Encore Azalea Versatility Sheet

nature-hills.title: Firepower Heavenly Bamboo
nature-hills.tags: gorgias_do_not_recommend, Low Maintenance, oos-channel-removed, out-of-stock, preorder, type_bush
nature-hills.body_html:
Add some power to your landscape with the Firepower Heavenly Bamboo (Nandina domestica 'Firepower') and its incredible year-round color! Your Firepower Nandina packs a lot of color into a small package. New growth emerges light green in spring, slowly transitioning to darker tones. By late summer, red tints grace the leaves as a preview of what's to come.

Also known as Chinese Sacred Bamboo, Firepower is a broadleaf evergreen shrub with outstanding fall and winter intensity! Plant one for an incredible display, or scatter a few around your yard. The dwarf Firepower is an easy shrub to find space for, and its amazing scarlet tones make it ideal as a way to bring a smattering of color to your yard for fall or winter.

When autumn arrives, the true splendor of this Heavenly Bamboo variety is revealed when all the "firepower" of this remarkable shrub is unleashed in an explosion of scarlet color that will set your yard ablaze. Perhaps most impressive is the way Firepower maintains its vibrant, red fall appearance into the barren winter months.

Remaining evergreen in warmer USDA planting zones 6 to 11, in cooler zones Nandina are semi-evergreen or act as deciduous shrubs, growing 2-3 feet tall and wide. Urban pollution-tolerant, disease, and pest-resistant plants, Nandina has an upright, compact nature, far smaller than that of other varieties. Firepower won't require any special care from you, and it's even drought-tolerant once established!

All this adaptability means in some locations, unfortunately, Nandina pops up where it should not. Enter your Zip Code in the Plant Highlights section to find your Hardiness Zone on this page and you'll see if Nature Hills can ship Nandina safely into your area. While Firepower doesn't bloom much and is mostly fruitless, birds can still spread the seeds that do form.

Nature Hills Nursery is at the forefront of environmental regulatory compliance with all agricultural laws. We use an online tool called Plant Sentry™, which blocks shipments of plants that can behave invasively in your locale.

Planting and Application:

Firepower transforms color beginning in spring and lasting until fall! Always tidy and showing off its compact leafy cane-like stems, this lovely foliage plant sets your landscape ablaze! This tidy broadleaf evergreen shrub will be perfect for your foundation plantings, as low hedges, and as property division with carefree maintenance.

Dwarf Heavenly Bamboo shrubs like Firepower radiate brilliantly as a container planting, adding lacy texture and color to your mixed planters as filler and thrillers, or as the specimen planting in a concrete pot or half whiskey barrel! Create conversation and screening around your seating areas, add a touch of tropical beauty around the pool, or dress up your balcony!

The lacy texture and space-saving size pop as a border row or edging! Try adding color to the weary row of conifers you have along your foundation hedge, both hiding their bare leggy lower stems as a facer plant, dwarf Nandia will also bring in that year-round color! Long low, winding hedges or en masse plantings will surround your landscape or your business in low-maintenance curb appeal that will turn heads each season!

  • Light Green Foliage In Spring Ages Darker Green & Red
  • 4-Season Interest Broadleaf Evergreen With Fall & Winter Color
  • Few Tiny White Flowers & Rarely Produces The Red Berries
  • Dwarf Low-Growing Shrub With Lacy Foliage
  • Low Hedges, Edging, Foundation Plantings, En Masse & Container Thrillers

#ProPlantTips for Care:

These rugged evergreen plants are easy to care for and grow in full sun or shaded conditions. In very hot areas, give them shade in the afternoon. Nandina needs well-drained soil, and water carefully to establish, and provide supplemental water only during long periods of drought from then on. Add 3-4 inches of arborist mulch over the root system to keep it cool.

Nandina is a beautiful plant on many 'firescaping or firewise' plant lists. Their drought tolerance makes Nandina ideal for Rock Gardens and Xeric plantings! Prune to shape once a year after flowering.

  • Best Color In Full Sun & Some Afternoon Shade
  • Any Well-Drained Soil Type
  • Moderate to Low Moisture - Drought Tolerant Once Established
  • Prune If Needed After Flowering
  • Low-Maintenance & Disease, Pest, Heat & Drought Resistance

If you love Nandina but want something with more 'oomph', then Firepower Nandina may be the variety for you. This incredible little shrub will not only fit in just about anywhere but impress you with how its small shape can hold such a huge amount of vibrant color. Order one for your garden today from NatureHills.com!

planting-tree.title: Heavenly Bamboo Nandina
planting-tree.tags: Bloom Color_Red, Category_Disease Resistant, Category_Evergreen Shrubs, Category_Nandina, Category_Shrubs, Deer Resistant, Drought Tolerance_Good, Fall Color_Red, Gifts, Grows in Zones_10, Grows in Zones_6, Grows in Zones_7, Grows in Zones_8, Grows in Zones_9, Mature Height_3-6 Feet, Mature Width_3-4 Feet, Nandina domestica, Shipping Restriction_AK, Shipping Restriction_AZ, Shipping Restriction_FL, Shipping Restriction_HI, Shipping Restriction_OR, Soil Type_Adaptable, Sunlight_Full, Sunlight_Partial
planting-tree.body_html:
The Heavenly Bamboo Nandina has the most phenomenal year-round color and interest that you can find in a shrub. This evergreen plant offers stunning Asian appeal similar to bamboo without all the issues that come with real bamboo.

Young foliage emerges with a red or pink tint and changes to light green as it matures. Cool-weather brings out vivid red color. Hints of red over green begins in fall and often deepens to dark red in late fall or winter.

Clusters of pinkish-white flowers appear in spring and are followed up by bright, cherry red berries that persist into winter. The bamboo-like upright branches of this nandina offer even more interest as they often acquire the same red color the leaves gain in autumn

The Heavenly Bamboo Nandina grows in partial and full sun and is adaptable to just about any well-drained soil. This plant thrives on minimal care and requires no pruning. Nadinas are pest and disease resistant. After establishing Heavenly Bamboo Nandinas are very drought tolerant. We recommend fertilizing with our balanced, slow-release fertilizer when planting and in early spring for optimal growth.

Add a colorful and uniquely exotic look to your home or garden with the Heavenly Bamboo Nandina. Order yours today!

nature-hills.title: Santa Rosa Plum Tree
nature-hills.tags: gorgias_do_not_recommend, oos-channel-removed, out-of-stock, preorder, type.tree_fruit, WNI
nature-hills.body_html:
Heat-Tolerant Gorgeous Juicy Fruit - Santa Rosa Plum Tree!

  • Delicious Healthful Fruit
  • Gorgeous Red-Hued Purple Skin
  • Sweet Amber Yellow Flesh
  • Fragrant Pinkish-White Spring Flowers
  • Late Mid-Season
  • Dependable Harvest Year After Year
  • Hardy, Heat-Tolerant
  • Self-Fertile
  • Healthy Versatile Jewels
  • Full Sun!

Treat yourself to in-season Plums in arms reach, when you plant or Santa Rosa Plum Tree (Prunus 'Santa Rosa') in your yard. First introduced right here in the United States by Luther Burbank, in 1906 in Santa Rosa, California!

This variety is a deciduous, fruit-bearing tree that offers a dependable and delicious harvest each year. Plant one where you can readily enjoy its beautiful blossoms and its gorgeous, juicy fruit.

Watch each spring as the Santa Rosa Plum's fabulously fragrant spring blooms, in frilly white flowers with a pink blush, make their way into your landscape. You'll create a visual spectacle that will have the whole neighborhood jealous.

Enjoy the show while you can; by late July or early August, those blossoms will give way to lovely Plums, ripe for the taking. Santa Rosa Japanese Plums are beautiful to behold, with red-hued, purple skin concealing a layer of red blush, then sweet, yellow flesh surrounding the center clingstone pit.

How to Use Santa Rosa Plum Tree

Your Santa Rosa Plum Tree will produce medium-large, slightly firm fruits that are ideal for fresh-eating right off the tree. Slice them for inclusion in a fruit salad, or add them to a fruit snack plate.

These beautiful Plums are also perfect for canning, freezing, cooking, drying, so you can easily preserve some of your bounty for enjoying later on in the year or sharing with others as gifts. Santa Rosa's firm flesh has amazing flavor! It is sweet with a slightly tangy edge, perfect for a wide variety of uses.

Edible landscaping is all the rage, so remember these gorgeous trees serve double duty as both an ornamental flowering tree that just happens to also grow healthful fruit! Plum leaves are lush and also have great fall color. Even the branching is ornate and provides winter interest!

Faster growing and vigorous, these trees are amazingly heat resistant and do great up to USDA growing zones 9, while being cold hardy down to zone 5! A great size for both a front yard accent tree and for easy use in the orchard!

#ProPlantTips For Care

All fruit trees do their best in full sun, at least 6-8 hours of uninterrupted sunlight. This gives you the highest amount of blooms and therefore the most fruit and the best tasting Plums possible.

Needing ample access to moisture, especially during fruit formation, Plums prefer well-drained soils. A generous layer of mulch helps retain soil moisture as well as insulate the root system from heat and chill.

Santa Rosa Plum Tree is a relatively hardy tree, adaptable to a variety of soils and heat tolerant. It's even self-fruitful, so you don't need to have more than one to enjoy a healthy harvest. Santa Rosa only requires about 300-500 chill hours a year to fruit.

However, you will get more fruit production when planted with other Japanese varieties as pollinator partners. Try Methley or Burgundy to amplify your harvest.

Santa Rosa is an old favorite among Plum lovers and still one of the most often grown. A pretty tree, especially during the spring and late summer growing seasons, Santa Rosa a great investment in something that is both useful and beautiful. Order yours today!

planting-tree.title: Santa Rosa Plum
planting-tree.tags: Bloom Color_White, Category_Fruit Trees, Category_Fruits & Berries, Category_Plum Tree, Deer Resistant, Drought Tolerance_Good, Grows in Zones_5, Grows in Zones_6, Grows in Zones_7, Grows in Zones_8, Grows in Zones_9, Mature Height_15-20 Feet, Mature Width_15-20 Feet, Shipping Restriction_AK, Shipping Restriction_AR, Shipping Restriction_AZ, Shipping Restriction_CA, Shipping Restriction_CO, Shipping Restriction_HI, Shipping Restriction_ID, Shipping Restriction_LA, Shipping Restriction_OR, Shipping Restriction_WA, Soil Type_Adaptable, Sunlight_Full
planting-tree.body_html:
The cultivar was introduced by plant breeder Luther Burbank in 1906 and is considered a producer of high-quality fruit. Though replaced by many commercial growers with more bacteria-resistant varieties, "Santa Rosa" is still a favorite among home gardeners and is often found at farmer’s markets.

A showy display of white flowers in spring yields an abundant harvest of dark crimson fruit each summer. Firm flesh with a wonderful flavor for fresh eating or in preserves. Fruit production is higher when another plum tree is nearby. A lovely border tree or garden accent.

How To Care

Once you get your new plum trees, keep them in their pots and place them in a covered, shady spot outside, like a back porch. Leave them there for 3 to 4 hours and gradually increase the time spent outside by 1 to 2 hours per day. Bring them back indoors each night.

Water deeply and frequently in extreme heat conditions. You may occasionally mist the leaves with water if the environment indoors is drier than outdoors.

The Santa Rosa plum tree is one of the most popular trees to select as a pollen source for other Asian plum trees.

Landscape Ideas

The plums are medium to large in size and have red-purple skin and yellow flesh that changes to red near the skin. They are juicy and provide a balanced blend of sweet and tart. These beautiful plums are also perfect for canning, freezing, cooking, drying, so you can easily preserve some of your bounty for enjoying later on in the year or sharing with others as gifts. Santa Rosa's flavor is sweet with a slight tangy edge, perfect for a variety of uses.

planting-tree.title: Dwarf Cavendish Banana Tree
planting-tree.tags: Bloom Color_Pink, Category_Banana Tree, Category_Dwarf Fruit Trees, Category_Fruit Trees, Category_Low Maintenance, Category_Ornamental Banana Plants, Category_Trees, Category_Tropical Fruit Tree, Category_Tropical Plants, Category_Tropical Trees, Drought Tolerance_Good, Grows in Zones_10, Grows in Zones_8, Grows in Zones_9, Indoor/Patio, Mature Height_6-10 Feet, Mature Width_6-10 Feet, Perfect for Pots, Pre-order Ship Date_2021-05-10, Shipping Restriction_AK, Shipping Restriction_AZ, Shipping Restriction_HI, Soil Type_Adaptable, Sunlight_Full, Sunlight_Partial
planting-tree.body_html:
Introduction

Grow Your Own Delicious Bananas

  • Dwarf fruit plant that grows in pots
  • Self-pollinating – you only need 1 plant!
  • Healthy fruit – rich in fiber, potassium, and vitamin C
  • Easy to grow and low maintenance
  • Enjoy a gorgeous tropical plant in your landscape or home

    Description

    Ever thought about growing your own bananas? With the Dwarf Cavendish Banana tree, this can be a reality. While this fruit tree is recommended for USDA growing zones 8 to 11, it can be grown as a patio plant in cooler climates. Simply move your plant inside in a sunny spot in winter.

    Bananas are healthy and yummy, but it isn’t just about tasty bananas, this plant is absolutely beautiful. With pretty green leaves that grow up to 4 feet long, the Dwarf Cavendish Banana tree adds an exotic, tropical look to your landscape or patio. This dwarf fruit tree stays compact and looks great in containers.

    Key Features

    • Dwarf fruit tree. This small banana tree only grows 8 to 10 feet tall and 6 to 8 feet wide at (in-ground) maturity. It will stay smaller when grown in pots.
    • Self-fertile. You can grow bananas with only one Dwarf Cavendish Banana plant.
    • Healthy Fruit. Bananas are great for your health. They are chock full of vitamins, minerals, and fiber. Eating bananas is good for your gut, heart, eyes, and bones. Bananas can even help with depression, mood, and weight loss.
    • Easy to grow. These fruit plants rarely have problems with pests or disease. They are drought tolerant and low maintenance once established and are easy to grow without harsh chemicals.
    • Attractive plant. The Cavendish Banana tree looks absolutely stunning in a home landscape. The tropical feel adds major appeal, especially around a pool or patio.

    Buy a Dwarf Cavendish Banana Tree for sale today! We love these fast-growing banana plants and we know you will too. Order yours today and start growing your own bananas!

    How To Care

    Bananas trees prefer full sun and well-drained, nutrient-rich soil.

    Water your Dwarf Cavendish Banana when the soil begins to dry.

    Banana plants grow fast and love to eat. Feed your Dwarf Cavendish Banana tree with our slow-release fertilizer twice per year and more often if your banana tree is grown in a pot.

    Prune back any leaves that prevent developing fruit from receiving fun sun. Remove any dead leaves. After a stalk has produced fruit, prune it back to the ground. This stalk can be sliced and used as mulch.

    Landscape Ideas

    The Dwarf Cavendish Banana tree grows quickly, but only reaches 8 to 10 feet tall at maturity. It grows well in containers and is a beautiful tropical plant. The long leaves add great texture and beauty to your landscape.

    Plant these tropical fruit trees around your pool or patio for an amazing look. You’ll love having a tropical getaway in your own backyard!

    Browse out Dwarf Fruit Trees and Tropical Trees for similar options.
